meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap by building a SaaS pricing engine. Master real-world architecture with Antigravity and Claude Code today.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' block is a failure of scope, not creativity. Solve it by building a dynamic SaaS pricing calculator. This project forces you to model real business logic, database relationships, and UI states, transforming a blank screen into a professional, portfolio-ready asset that demonstrates immediate commercial value.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the weight of a thousand tutorials pressing down on you. You've mastered the syntax, but you're trapped in 'tutorial hell'—caught between the desire to build and the paralyzing lack of a concrete, real-world project. The problem isn't your skill; it's your focus. You need a project that mimics the complexity of a real enterprise environment.

Enter the SaaS Pricing Engine. It’s the perfect intermediate project because it forces you to bridge the gap between abstract code and actual business utility. By building a system that calculates tiers, handles user-specific discounts, and updates in real-time, you aren't just coding—you're learning how to architect revenue-generating software.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Logic Model
steps.description: Before writing code, establish your data schema. We need to handle tiered pricing (Basic, Pro, Enterprise) and dynamic modifiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including base price, feature flags, and recurring billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a schema.prisma file for a User, Subscription, and PricingPlan model.'

steps.title: Build the Logic Engine
steps.description: Construct the core calculation engine that determines the final price based on user inputs and selected add-ons.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pure JavaScript function that accepts a base plan and an array of feature toggles, returning a calculated monthly total.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create a pricingService.ts file that exports a calculateTotal function with unit tests.'

steps.title: Implement Interactive UI
steps.description: Create a responsive interface that updates the pricing display instantly when the user interacts with toggles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component using Tailwind CSS that displays pricing tiers and a dynamic total price summary.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a PricingTable.tsx component with state management for selected plan and feature add-ons.'

steps.title: Add Persistence and API Layer
steps.description: Connect your engine to a backend API to save the user's selected plan to a database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Next.js API route that validates a pricing selection and updates the user's subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement a POST /api/subscribe route that integrates with the pricingService logic.'

steps.title: Deploy and Portfolio Integration
steps.description: Finalize your build and prepare it for your public portfolio to demonstrate professional-grade architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a README.md snippet explaining the technical architecture and business value of this pricing engine for a portfolio.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment script for Vercel and a professional project summary for GitHub.'

pro_tips: Always separate your business logic from the UI; this allows you to unit test your pricing calculations without needing a browser.
Use 'feature flags' in your database to toggle pricing tiers on/off without needing to redeploy your entire application.
Implement 'optimistic UI' updates for the pricing total to make the application feel instantaneous to the end-user.
Add a 'Price History' logging feature to track how changing your pricing model affects user conversion rates over time.
Use TypeScript branded types for money values to prevent accidental currency conversion errors in your calculation engine.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, extend it by integrating a payment gateway like Stripe to turn this project into a functional SaaS prototype.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build a professional-grade SaaS component that solves a real business problem. Don't just watch; execute, iterate, and deploy. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
